name: Create Article
description: Slug is generated in SQL (no slugify expr function exists — grep of internal/expr confirmed this; documented finding). Uniqueness is guaranteed by appending a value pulled from articles_id_seq (the BIGSERIAL sequence backing articles.id) to the kebab-cased title, all inside the single INSERT, so no separate uniqueness round-trip or retry is needed. tagList insert uses one db.exec with unnest($2::text[]) for both the tags upsert and the article_tags link (see 'insert_tags'); this is the canonical article-select's first use (see 'article' + 'author' nodes), reused verbatim by get/update-article and by later tasks.

config.query: INSERT INTO articles (slug, title, description, body, author_id) VALUES (lower(regexp_replace($1, '[^a-zA-Z0-9]+', '-', 'g')) || '-' || nextval('articles_id_seq'), $1, $2, $3, $4) RETURNING id
WITH ins_tags AS (INSERT INTO tags (name) SELECT unnest($2::text[]) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) INSERT INTO article_tags (article_id, tag) SELECT $1, unnest($2::text[]) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

SELECT a.slug, a.title, a.description, a.body, a.created_at, a.updated_at, a.author_id, to_json(COALESCE(array_agg(DISTINCT at.tag) FILTER (WHERE at.tag IS NOT NULL), '{}')) AS tag_list, (SELECT count(*) FROM favorites f WHERE f.article_id = a.id) AS favorites_count, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM favorites f WHERE f.article_id = a.id AND f.user_id = $2) AS favorited FROM articles a LEFT JOIN article_tags at ON at.article_id = a.id WHERE a.id = $1 GROUP BY a.id

SELECT metadata->>'username' AS username, metadata->>'bio' AS bio, metadata->>'image' AS image, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM follows fo WHERE fo.follower_id = $2 AND fo.followee_id = $1) AS following FROM auth_users WHERE id = $1
